weather_report:
Subiaco settles under a lid of cloud tonight, the earlier patchwork sky knitting itself into an even, charcoal quilt that hides the stars and softens the streetlights. Sunshine is finished for the day and the UV is effectively nil; dawn looks set to arrive muted, with overcast holding the light flat and silvery rather than bright. Expect a rather low-contrast morning—clear enough to see your way, but with edges blurred and colors subdued—until any stray breaks, if they appear at all, open a brief window of gentler sun.

It’s a night for easy layers: a light jacket or soft sweater if you’re stepping out, something you won’t mind carrying home if the air stays mild. Put the kettle on, portion tomorrow’s lunch, set your bag by the door, and let the evening idle. If you need a last circuit of the block, the sky will keep you company; if you’re up early, a quiet coffee by the window will suit the slow-brightening cloud.
